§6Cable Anchor§f:
The §6Cable Anchor §f(appliedenergistics2:part:120) is a §eutility cable part§f that lets an ME cable §battach to a single face§f of a block without forming a §6full network connection§f. It is a cosmetic and structural tool — it does not transfer power, items, or channels.

§6Production Path (network/parts/cable_anchor.json)§f:
Shapeless recipe with 2 ingredients:
- 1× §6Metal Ingot§f from the §6#appliedenergistics2:quartz_blockIngots §foredict tag (covers §6Iron Ingot§f, §6Copper Ingot§f, etc.)
- 1× §6Knife §ffrom the §6#appliedenergistics2:nether_quartz_cutting_knife §foredict tag (any mod's knife)
- Yields 3× Cable Anchor

§6Function (PartCableAnchor.java:48-213)§f:
- §bCable attachment§f: place the Cable Anchor on any §6ME Cable §f face; the cable is visually "anchored" to the adjacent block
- §bNo network join§f: the host block does §cnot §f connect to the ME network — the anchor is purely cosmetic
- §bLadder behaviour§f: the anchor acts as a §6climbable surface §f (PartCableAnchor.java:108-111) — `isLadder` returns true on a side face
- §bFacade support§f: when the host cable has a §6Facade §f on the anchor's side, the anchor uses the §6FACADE_MODELS §f variant (PartCableAnchor.java:204-211)
- §bNo redstone I/O§f: the anchor is §cnot §f a redstone source or sink

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the Cable Anchor is a component of, but it is referenced in 550 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bCable routing §f: keep a cable from forming an unwanted network branch
- §bAesthetic support §f: visually attach a cable to a wall
- §bLadder §f: free ladder for vertical shaft access

§6Placement (PartCableAnchor.java:200-202)§f:
- §eTip§f: the anchor can be placed on any §6ME Cable §f (glass, covered, smart, dense) but §cnot §f on §6ME Controller §f or §6ME Drive §f faces
- §eTip§f: use anchors to route cables along §6Quartz Glass §f facades for a clean industrial look
- §cWarning§f: a Cable Anchor consumes no power and provides no channel — it is §cnot §f a substitute for a §6ME Controller §f
- §cWarning§f: removing the anchor with a §6Wrench §f drops it; breaking the host cable will also break the anchor
